summary: Jeff Atwood announces a new CodeProject article introducing WebFileManager, a utility page for ASP.NET websites. The tool addresses a common developer pain point: managing files on servers you don't have direct access to. Rather than writing one-off pages for each file operation, WebFileManager provides a generic, reusable solution that can be dropped into any ASP.NET site. Atwood presents it modestly as practical rather than groundbreaking.
key_points: Developers often deploy to servers they don't control and lack filesystem access
A generic solution beats writing special-purpose pages for each file management need
Practical, reusable tools save time even if they're not technically innovative
Sharing tools with the community and soliciting feedback improves them
money_quotes: I often deploy ASP.NET websites to servers that I don't control.
Rather than writing a bunch of special purpose pages to deal with file management, I developed a generic WebFileManager page than can be dropped into any ASP.NET website.
Nothing earth shattering, but I find it very useful.
key_insight: Building generic, reusable tools for common problems—even unglamorous ones like file management—is more valuable than writing one-off solutions.
